params.name: Qin Bronze Chariot
params.description:
On Jul 22, 1978 AD, a giant object was discovered on the west side of the Qinling Mausoleum.
It was a replica made of bronze that was proportionally accurate to the real-life chariots and horses of the Qin Dynasty. The entire chariot and horse was made of more than 3,000 parts and is the largest unearthed ancient vehicle to date, with the most complex structure and complete carriage system. The components of the bronze chariot and horse were all cast and required different casting methods dep …
